sections.title: Starting out
Talking to Lazim

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ah, an adventurer. Could you spare some of your time to help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what's the matter?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: It's a sorry tale. I was once an accomplished sculptor, you see.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I made statues for the kings, queens and rulers of Gielinor. But then there was the accident...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: After that, I could never hold a chisel again.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: But I have been struck by my muse while travelling the desert, and want nothing more than to build a statue in this area.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: The desert's plain and full of sand, and needs more decoration.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what would you need my help with?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: As I said, I can't hold a chisel, but I can guide you in crafting the statue for me.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'd need you to quarry the stone for it, as well.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, will you help me with my art?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll have to explain it to me as you go along, but count me in.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Excellent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing to make your statue?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: There is a stone quarry nearby; I require several blocks of sandstone in order to make the statue.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Each of these blocks should be around... well, let's say thirty two kilograms for the first one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't mine a block that big!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, then, bring me enough smaller blocks to make thirty two kilograms, and I will deal with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, how much do different blocks of sandstone weigh?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: From what the last adventurer told me, there are tiny blocks that weigh 1 kg, small blocks that weigh 2 kg, and medium-sized blocks that weigh 5 kg.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: There are also large blocks that weigh 10 kg, but I doubt a weedy adventurer like you could even carry one, let alone mine one cleanly.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Apparently, the more skilled you are at mining, the larger the blocks you can chip off the rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get on with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, that sounds like a lot of work.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: All true art requires work.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim again before accepting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing to make your statue?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: There is a stone quarry nearby; I require several blocks of sandstone in order to make the statue.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Each of these blocks should be around... well, let's say thirty two kilograms for the first one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't mine a block that big!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, then, bring me enough smaller blocks to make thirty two kilograms, and I will deal with it.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim while holding sandstone for the base

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some sandstone for you!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, hand it over, then.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That makes [1-32] kg I'm carrying now.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'm now carrying [1-32] kg of your inexpertly-quarried stone.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That's [1-32] kg I have now.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I need [1-32] kg more stone to make the base. Any more stone you want to burden me down with?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I need [1-32] kg more stone to make the base. Have you any more to make me carry?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I need [1-32] kg more stone to make the base. Do you have any more stone you want to offload onto me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have more stone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a large 10 kg block.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Thank you, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a medium 5 kg block.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Oh, joy. I'm sure I'll treasure it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a small 2 kg block.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: So generous of you, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a tiny 1 kg block.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Either you've been splitting stone into smaller blocks, or you're worse at quarrying stone than I expected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why won't you take more than 32 kg of stone?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Because, adventurer, I'm not going to carry more of your blocks than I absolutely must.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: If your blocks are too big, break them with a chisel or a pick.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That makes [1-32] kg I'm carrying now.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'm now carrying [1-32] kg of your inexpertly-quarried stone.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That's [1-32] kg I have now.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That's enough for the base of the statue, certainly.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Here you are, adventurer. Now, use a chisel to craft it into a base for the statue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I supposed to carry that? It's huge!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Oh, stop whining. The last adventurer who helped me was carrying around a girl in a barrel.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Zamorak only knows why, but [he/she] didn't complain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that's all for now.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after getting the Sandstone (32kg)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this large block of sandstone you gave me...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I told you to carve it into the base of the statue, correct?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, you did.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Then why are you standing around brainlessly? Get on with it!

sections.title: Using chisel on the Sandstone (32kg)
Talking to Lazim with the Sandstone base

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made the base of the statue!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Excellent. Now, just put it on the flat ground next to me.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after placing the Sandstone base

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want me to do now?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Hmph. Well, the base of the statue is done, but it needs a body. Twenty kilograms should be enough for you to make it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do it right away!

sections.title: Talking to Lazim while holding sandstone for the body

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some sandstone for you!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, hand it over, then.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That makes [1-20] kg I'm carrying now.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'm now carrying [1-20] kg of your inexpertly-quarried stone.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That's [1-20] kg I have now.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I need [1-20] kg more stone to make the body. Any more stone you want to burden me down with?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I need [1-20] kg more stone to make the body. Have you any more to make me carry?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I need [1-20] kg more stone to make the body. Do you have any more stone you want to offload onto me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have more stone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a large 10 kg block.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Thank you, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a medium 5 kg block.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Oh, joy. I'm sure I'll treasure it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a small 2 kg block.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: So generous of you, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a tiny 1 kg block.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Either you've been splitting stone into smaller blocks, or you're worse at quarrying stone than I expected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why won't you take more than 32 kg of stone?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Because, adventurer, I'm not going to carry more of your blocks than I absolutely must.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: If your blocks are too big, break them with a chisel or a pick.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That makes [1-20] kg I'm carrying now.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'm now carrying [1-20] kg of your inexpertly-quarried stone.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That's [1-20] kg I have now.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: That should be enough to use for the statue's body.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: All you have to do with it, adventurer, is chisel it into the body of the statue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that's all for now.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim while holding the Sandstone (20kg)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this large block of sandstone you gave me.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Very nice, adventurer. You're supposed to make it into the statue's body.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Get your chisel ready and start working.

sections.title: Using chisel on the Sandstone (20kg)
Talking to Lazim while holding the Sandstone body

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made the statue's body!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well done, adventurer. Not as dense as you look.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Go on, add it to the base of the statue.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after using the body on the statue

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You utter incompetent! Have you added the body to the statue yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I have!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Hmm, then perhaps I'm still missing something...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want me to do next?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Oh, I don't know, adventurer. Go and chisel the statue a bit more, or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after chiseling the statue

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: It shouldn't have taken this long... did I miscalculate?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ah, adventurer. As you can see, the statue is nearly complete. All it needs is a head.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: The head should be made out of granite, which you can find in that quarry nearby.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: As for whose head... well, perhaps I'll let you impose your own artistic vision on it.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Whose head do you think should be on the statue?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it should have my head!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Are you mad?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You'd start carving it, then you'd take a helmet off or change your hairstyle, and nobody would know who on Gielinor it was supposed to be!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Statues are far more permanent than you adventurers and your fashions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can't put my head on the statue?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: No. Now choose something sensible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it should have your head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: After all, it's your statue.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Why, that's very generous of you, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Are you hoping for a bigger reward for saying that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, maybe.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Wonderful. Well, at least you have me here for reference.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Now, go and find a suitable block of granite and make that head.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: And do try to remember which one it is you're carving.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it should have a god's head.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: And which god were you thinking of? There's very little reference material here in the desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's true.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whose head do you think I should make?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, some of the people in the desert worship a god called Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Or, if you prefer, I'm sure you could sculpt Zamorak instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll use Zamorak's head for the statue.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ah, an excellent choice.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Now you should find a suitable block of granite, and make that head.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Try to remember which head you've decided to carve - I know you adventurers. Memories like fishing nets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll use Icthlarin's head for the statue.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: An interesting choice - I'm sure there's some reference material you can use around here.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Find a suitable block of granite, and get carving.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: And make sure to remember which one you're supposed to be carving. I've known some of you adventurers to forget things you were told three seconds ago.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: A human statue with a camel's head? Is there any particular reason?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, no. I just thought it would be funny.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, it is appropriate, all things considered...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Very well, it'll work well in a desert setting. Now, find a suitable block of granite, and carve that head.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Make sure you remember which head you're carving. The last adventurer who tried to help me forgot half- way through carving, and produced the strangest mixture of camel and human I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, I've done something like this before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had to help rebuild a statue in the city of Keldagrim, and then choose whose head should be placed on the top!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Keldagrim? Hmph. Technically good sculptors, the dwarves, but they have no sense of art.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'm sure that none of them would even think of travelling to the desert and building a statue here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I use the head I chose for that statue on yours?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Do you even remember what it looked like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... no.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Then no, you can't. Now, choose something else.

sections.title: Using chisel on the Granite (5kg)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. Which head did I decide to use for the statue?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There, that looks good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There, that looks good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was it supposed to look like that?

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after choosing a head
Talking to Lazim after creating a head

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a head for the statue, but...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure it's the right one.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Let me see...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You decided to make the statue's head look like me, you said.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Hmm. I don't think much of your artistic skills, adventurer, but it's a passable likeness.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: ...You really have a shorter attention span than a grain of sand, don't you?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You said you'd make the statue's head look like me, not like modern art!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'll remember next time.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Perhaps it was too difficult for you. Do you want to change what head you'll make?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'll stay with the one I chose first.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Fair enough. Remember, you're carving my head for the statue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'd like to change my choice.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, which will you choose this time?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You said you'd make the statue's head look like Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Hmm. You're not a particularly good sculptor, adventurer, but it's at least recognisable.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Do you even remember who you were supposed be carving?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: It was supposed to be Zamorak, not the horrific monstrosity you've carved!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I thought that was what he looked like.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: How dare you?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ahem, that is to say, er...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, it was obviously too tough for you. Do you want to change what head you'll make?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, you said that you'd make the statue's head look like Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You're not much of an artist, but at least you did your research. It'll pass.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Weren't you supposed to be carving Icthlarin's head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...I was?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, you're paying lots of attention, aren't you? Honestly, you adventurers.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: It would be easier on your poor little brain if you changed what head you'll make, don't you agree?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You said you'd make the statue's head look like a camel, and that looks like...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, it wouldn't fool a camel, but it's close enough for the statue.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I don't believe it. You said you were planning to make a camel head, but what's this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, not a camel head?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Correct, adventurer! Have a cake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Of course not! Why would I give something to you for having a worse memory than a piece of burnt fish?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Do you want to change what head you'll make to something you have a chance of remembering?

sections.title: Using the head on the statue before talking to Lazim

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Hold it! I know you adventurers.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I bet you've managed to make the wrong head, or something like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know what I'm doing, honest...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I don't care if you think you know what you're doing. Let me have a look at that head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, fine...

sections.title: Using the head on the statue after confirming it with Lazim

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ouch!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh, my duodenum!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow... Lazim has some explaining to do!

sections.title: Bottom floor of Enakhra's Temple
Talking to Lazim inside the temple

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ah, excellent, it's all gone as planned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gone as planned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We just fell through the ground into some weird temple!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Of course we did! That's why I had you build a statue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think you really are a sculptor. What are you looking for in this temple?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Oh, nothing. No treasure here. No priceless artefacts from the lost ages of Gielinor, or anything like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be leaving, then.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Wait! If you help me, then...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'll give you a share of whatever we find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds better. So, where's the treasure?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: At the top of the temple. We've fallen through to the very bottom, so you'll have to climb up to the next floor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good, but...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get further into the temple?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: The stories I've heard say there are odd doors in this temple, that have locks that don't need keys, but... rather large limbs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yuck!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You can use your chisel or pick to take them from the statue. They should be the right size.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Be thankful they're the statue's limbs and not yours, adventurer.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get further into the temple?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: The stories I've heard say there are odd doors in this temple, that have locks that don't need keys, but... rather large limbs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yuck!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You can use your chisel or pick to take them from the statue. They should be the right size.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Be thankful they're the statue's limbs and not yours, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where the statue's head is?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ah, it fell off when the statue dropped through the ceiling. I've been keeping it safe since then.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Try not to lose it, adventurer.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after getting the stone head once

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where the statue's head is?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You didn't get far from here before you mislaid it.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: This time, try not to drop it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where the statue's head is?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: ...You're carrying it, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Been at the Asgarnian Ale a little too much, have we?

sections.title: Using chisel on the Fallen statue
Talking to Lazim while holding the stone limbs

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you make these stone limbs easier to carry?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're really heavy, and they weigh me down.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Of course not! I'm not a carnival conjurer.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Adversity builds character, adventurer. Heft them around by yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after losing the stone limbs

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mislaid the statue's right arm.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I suppose I'm useful to have around at these times. I found it when you dropped it around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't find the statue's left arm.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: It's lucky for you that I notice these things. Here, you managed to drop it in this room.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I don't know where I put the statue's left leg.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You dropped it near the statue. Do I have to hold your hand through everything?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Here it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the statue's right leg.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, it didn't go far - I found it in here. Take better care of it next time.

sections.title: Open door to the north

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow, my head...

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: At last, it's complete. A temple fit for my lord.

sections.title: Take-sigil from Pedestal
Open door to the north-west

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow, my head...

lines.speaker: Knight
lines.text: All right, men, destroy that evil sorceress... and her temple with her!

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: You will never have this temple - it belongs to my lord!

lines.speaker: Knight
lines.text: F-for Avarrocka!

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Ah ha ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Knight
lines.text: Aaaaaargh!

sections.title: Open door to the south-west

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow, my head...

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: Well, why would Zamorak want a temple? You said he needed weapons instead.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: I wish I'd never supported him.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: So you've seen sense? You'll join our side?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Heh heh heh...

sections.title: Open door to the south-east

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow, my head...

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Surely this would make a good weapon...

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: No, no! Curse it, that doesn't work!

sections.title: Placing sigil in the door
Middle floor of Enakhra's Temple
Talking to Lazim after going upstairs

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with this pedestal I've found?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: What pedestal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's on the floor above this. It looks sort of important.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Really? Did it have some sort of recess on the top?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that you mention it, it did...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: In that case, perhaps you should try using the head of the statue on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where the statue's head is?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ah, it fell off when the statue dropped through the ceiling. I've been keeping it safe since then.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: ...You're carrying it, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Been at the Asgarnian Ale a little too much, have we?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You didn't get far from here before you mislaid it.

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after trying to use the statue's head on the pedestal

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with this pedestal I've found?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: What pedestal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's on the floor above this. It looks sort of important.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Really? Did it have some sort of recess on the top?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and I tried using the statue's head with it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...but it didn't fit properly.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Hmm. Intriguing, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Perhaps you should find out what shape the recess is, so you can sculpt something that will fit in it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure that will work?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Adventurer, I know more about this temple than you do.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: All you need to do is make a mould of the recess somehow, then use it as a guide to make another head.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: It should be made from the same granite as the statue's head was.

sections.title: Using soft clay on the pedestal
Using chisel on Granite (5kg) while holding the mould
Using the stone head (cavity) on the pedestal

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow, not another headache...

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: But it's still a nice temple, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: Wait... What is this?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Ha ha ha. You believed me, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: I would never desert my lord!

sections.title: Talking to Lazim on the middle floor

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well done unlocking those doors, adventurer. Did you have any problems?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I saw some really strange visions. Everything went grey...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Those are some of the traps to keep people away from this temple.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Thankfully, they didn't send you mad this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, are we at the top of the temple now?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: No, there is another floor above this. You have to go up a ladder in the room to the north.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hah, that's easy. Treasure, here I come!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: There's a magic barrier in the way. And right now, it won't let anything through.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any other way to get upstairs?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: No, but I suspect the other rooms on this floor have magical properties. If you solve all the puzzles they present, then...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will I know when I've solved them?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Oh, it'll be quite obvious.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: The globes on the pedestal here will also light when you've solved the room they correspond to.

sections.title: Talking to Pentyn

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Have you come to torture me again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Oh, you may look human, but I know you're not. I've heard stories.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: How you Mahjarrat can take on any form, and use magic to inflict pain without any weapons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, er... what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no idea what you're talking about, but I'm just as human as you are.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: You'll never get what you want from me. I'm a soldier of Avarrocka, and will stand strong!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I'm not going to hurt you!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Besides, I am already wounded, and have been so for many years, thanks to this spell you cast on me. And hungry. So very, very hungry.

sections.title: Using food on Pentyn

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some food you can have. It's quite tasty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, try this. It should fill you up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have some of this food. It's really good, I promise!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: My daughter was learning to cook this when I left.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I haven't remembered her in so long... thank you for reminding me of her.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: My son loved this dish. He would have eaten it every day if we had let him.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I haven't remembered him in so long... thank you for reminding me of him.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: A cake? There's nothing to celebrate, is there?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: So many years gone by. My children must have grown up and left home.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I haven't remembered them in so long... thank you for reminding me of them.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Did you just dig that up and throw it into an oven?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: It doesn't look good at all.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'd prefer it with some sort of topping...

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: That doesn't look too bad at all.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: It could almost be my wife's wonderful cooking.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I haven't remembered her in so long... thank you for reminding me of her.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: What do you take me for? A rabbit?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: What's this? Did you just dig it out of the ground?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: What do you think I am, a bear? It's not even seasoned!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'm a civilised man, not a barbarian gnawing on his kill!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Oh, how pleasant. I'm surprised you remembered to cook it after you killed it!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'm hungry, but not so hungry that I'll eat your leftovers.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: That's revolting! Why would you give people food you've already eaten half of?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'm not touching that!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I might have enjoyed that food if you hadn't already eaten part of it.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: So, you're throwing scraps of food to me like I'm a dog, are you?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: If only you could feel the bite of my sword!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Do you think I'm a gnome, or something? I'm not going to eat that!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: What are you trying to feed me? I don't want that!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Are you just trying to feed me everything you have?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: What do you take me for? A goat who'll eat whatever's in front of him?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: What are you trying to do, kill me?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Oh, I get it, you're experimenting on me with different things to see what I'll eat.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'm not going to eat something your cat dragged in!

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'd rather have some good human home baking.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Some fresh home baking would be delicious.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'd like something that's had some care put into making it.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'd like to have a hearty meal fresh from the oven.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you feeling better now?

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Yes, thank you.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: The food you gave me healed my wounds, and it also reminded me of my family; that helped me to remember who I was.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'm afraid that I'm trapped here, but I still remember something of this floor of the temple.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: If you need any hints about the rooms nearby, I can give you some help.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: When we came to the temple, that fountain was frosted with ice.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: Even chipping at it with swords or lighting fires under it didn't do anything.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: My guess is that some sort of magic is keeping it that way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the room with the furnace.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I tried to go in there, but the smoke was so thick I could barely see the furnace.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: It may be that it's blocked, in which case you'll have to clear it out.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: A good breeze should do the trick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the dark room.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I never went in there myself, but I peered through the door.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: There seemed to be fires of some sort all around the room, but they were burning low.

lines.speaker: Pentyn
lines.text: I'd imagine they've gone out by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, I don't need any help.

sections.title: Cast Fire Bolt, Blast, Wave, or Surge on Crust of ice
Investigating Braziers
Using items on Braziers
Casting Wind Bolt, Blast, Wave, or Surge on Furnace grate
Pass through Magic barrier
Talking to Lazim after unlocking the gate

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: The treasure's up ahead, adventurer. Be careful of the traps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What traps?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Oh, I don't know. Perhaps, say, huge skeletons that'll tear your arms and legs off?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Nothing, adventurer, nothing. You just trot along and get that treasure, now.

sections.title: Talking to the first Boneguard

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Leave this temple at once, or I shall be forced to attack!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I will defend this door until I am released from this prison of bone...

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: You shall not pass me, unless I crumble into dust!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: These bones that trap me will block your path!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Now, flee before I attack you!

sections.title: Casting crumble undead on the Boneguard

lines.speaker: Freed Spirit
lines.text: Thank you, kind [sir/lady]... now I am free from my imprisonment.

lines.speaker: Freed Spirit
lines.text: I no longer need these bones - take them, please, and bury what you can.

sections.title: Talking to Enakhra before the second Boneguard

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Don't interrupt me, human! I have to concentrate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: I'm keeping a weapon under control. If my attention wavers...

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: ...then it tries to escape me. I must keep it safe for my lord Zamorak.

sections.title: Talk to the second Boneguard

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Leave this place, before I am forced to attack!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: If you value your life, young [man/woman/one], then flee!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: You should not be here! This is a dangerous place for beings such as you!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I am impressed, young [man/woman/one]. You seem to have more resilience than those I have fought off before.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Perhaps, then, you could help me with my predicament?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what's wrong?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I am trapped in this form against my will... it has been a long and painful struggle to even regain control over my own mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you attack me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't do anything to deserve it!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: As I said, I am trapped like this against my will.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I am forced to defend the one who enslaved me from any harm, even though it disgusts me to do so!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's trapped you in this form?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: SHE has. She was my rival before we came to this world, and supported that upstart Zamorak in his quest to be a god.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: It angers me to think at how she tricked me into slavery...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I understand, but who is 'she'?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Her name is Enakhra, and she is the one who made this accursed temple and this boneguard that I am trapped in.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Now, since you show some interest in my situation, will you help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, I'll help you out. But first of all, do you have any treasure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, er, was just wondering...

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Certainly, young [man/woman/one]. If you help me, I will give you a valuable amulet, and some of my knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good enough for me. What do I have to do?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Enakhra keeps me trapped in this form with magic.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Her hold over me grew weaker over the years, and I began to build a wall to keep her trapped in her own temple forever.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Unfortunately, she has strengthened her spells lately, and I can no longer will myself to continue building.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I want you to finish building this wall, that we might seal her in this temple forever, where she cannot taint the world with her creations again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About this wall...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get the stone?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: There is a pile of rubble nearby, made of blocks that have fallen from the walls and the ceiling over time.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I have been trapped here for several thousand years, after all...

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Is there anything else you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I build the wall?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Since the blocks are heavy, you will need to be quite strong in order to lift them up to the wall.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Once you have placed the stones up on the wall, you must trim them with a chisel so that they do not fall off when you place the next layer.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Is there anything else you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long will it take?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I was not far from finishing, I must say. It will most likely only take you three loads to finish building it.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Is there anything else you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about Enakhra?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: She is a coward, and a despicable traitor. She even supported Zamorak when he tried to become a god.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Of course, even though she built a temple for him, he didn't care, and she's been hiding here ever since.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Is there anything else you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about yourself?

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I was once a proud mage, and now look at me. Trapped inside a bone skeleton and forced to defend my rival's temple.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: To think I believed her when she said she wanted to become allies and make amends for our rivalry!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: That two-faced, conniving, backstabbing wretch...

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I suppose it is our nature, after all.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Is there anything else you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll start building.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Thank you. Once you are finished, the wall itself will shield me from most of her magic, and I will be free to move once more.

sections.title: Talking to Enakhra after the second Boneguard

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: So, you're determined to meddle in other people's business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: That weapon has suddenly become a lot harder to control.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: And I know it's your doing. He's tried to get you to help, hasn't he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're talking about Akthanakos, then yes!

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Of course, I don't suppose he's told you who he's trying to support, has he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, no. But if you hate him so much, I suppose he doesn't support Zamorak...

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: How dare you throw around his name so casually?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: No, Akthanakos never supported Zamorak. Not even after these thousands of years...

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: He has pledged his allegiance to someone much more foul.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: ...There are some names it is wiser not to mention, human.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Such as the name of my lord Zamorak, that you seem so fond of throwing around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you said 'Zamorak' first!

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Out of my sight, you unworthy scum, before I rend your flesh from your bones.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Never dishonour his name again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

sections.title: Talking to Lazim after before building the wall

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, adventurer, do you have the treasure yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There doesn't seem to be any treasure there.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: None at all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... the big skeleton promised to give me something if I helped him.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ah ha! So, it's not attacking you? Well done, adventurer. Your second brain cell must be working overtime today.

sections.title: Using sandstone on the wall
Using chisel on the wall
Finishing the wall

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: No! What have you done?

sections.title: Talking to the Boneguard after finishing the wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The wall's finally built!

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: Thank you. Thank you very much for all your help.

lines.speaker: Boneguard
lines.text: I can feel Enakhra's control over me fading. Finally, I can be free from this imprisonment...

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: After thousands of years... finally, I am no longer her slave.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: Honour demands that I reward you... and yet you have no room to carry my reward. Please, return when you can take it from me.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: As thanks for your help, young [man/woman/one], please take this amulet of mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. Er, what does it do?

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: It allows you to understand the speech of the Ugthanki, my patron animals, and other camels.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: It is also bound to the desert, and can thus transport you back to this temple if you wish.

sections.title: Talking to Akthanakos before finishing the cutscene

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: After thousands of years... finally, I am no longer her slave.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: Honour demands that I reward you... and yet you have no room to carry my reward. Please, return when you can take it from me.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: As thanks for your help, young [man/woman/one], please take this amulet of mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. Er, what does it do?

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: It allows you to understand the speech of the Ugthanki, my patron animals, and other camels.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: It is also bound to the desert, and can thus transport you back to this temple if you wish.

sections.title: Enakhra cutscene

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: After so many years, why didn't you just give up?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Now my lord will punish you for destroying his weapon.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: Ha ha... in that case, I'm sure lord Zaros will be happy to intervene.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: I'll be waiting for you in the north, Akthanakos.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: It'll be a pleasure to fight you once more...

sections.title: Post-quest
Talking to Lazim after the quest

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well, thank you, adventurer. You've just deprived me of a wonderful chance to learn from one of Zamorak's first followers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? What about that treasure you were looking for?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You utter imbecile! Did you think I was looking for gold?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I want knowledge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... sorry.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: 'Sorry' won't bring those Mahjarrat back.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: *sigh* I might as well stay to study this temple. It has some interesting magical properties.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ooh, you're a mage? Can you teach me any spells?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'm sure I've dropped hints the size of Varrock, but you might not have noticed. I'm a Zamorakian mage.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: If you really want to learn my spells, you'll have to dedicate yourself to Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I couldn't do that!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Then you won't be learning any of my spells.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Ha ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not very nice, are you?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: What did you expect from a Zamorakian mage?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You really thought I would teach you my secrets just like that? Ha ha ha! You are a naive fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not very nice of you.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'm a mage of Zamorak, after all. What else do you expect?

sections.title: Talking to Lazim again after the quest

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: How are you and your defective brain doing nowadays, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the amulet that Akthanakos gave me.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You scatterbrained fool. You dropped it in here. Since you have no space to carry it, I can't give it back yet.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: You really are an idiot, aren't you? You dropped it in here when you left. Try not to lose it next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! That's not very nice.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: I'm not very nice. If you want to see who ruined all of my plans, adventurer, just look in a mirror.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Suit yourself. At least I got a nice amulet for my trouble.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: And don't forget who gave it unlimited charges for you!

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: About that amulet - I might be able to improve it for you, for a fee. Have you got it with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have it on me at the moment. I must have stored it somewhere.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Of course you did. I bet you spend your life collecting junk, storing it, then complaining you've run out of storage space. Fetch the amulet if you want my help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got it here.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Yes, it's what I thought. This amulet will run out of charges all the time, if you keep using it. For one million coins I can give it unlimited charges.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you need so much money for it?

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Three reasons. Firstly, I'm a Zamorakian mage - we're not big on charity work. Secondly, I want the money. Finally, in case you need reminding, I hate you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Well, I haven't got that kind of money on me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, here's one million coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not interested.

lines.speaker: Lazim
lines.text: Your loss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe another time.
